transcript: This IPL team was losing every season but still became a 1,000 cr lifestyle brand. Now it's in the top 10 biggest teams in the world on Instagram. Isala Cup Namu. While most franchises disappear for 10 months after IPL ends, RCB cracked the code of staying relevant 365 days a year. How they stopped thinking like a cricket team and started acting like a lifestyle empire. First they picked players with massive personalities. Next, they created physical spaces like the RCB bar and cafe in Bangalore, giving fans a place to live the RCB experience even when there's no cricket happening. title: Can A Wooden Board BEAT The IPad? 🫨
transcript: This boring toy looks like a chopping board, but they got every shark fighting to invest in their company. It all started in 2021 during the lockdown when Yash and Vidang noticed something very troubling. Screens were replacing parents. Kids simply couldn't go a moment without a device. Screens were becoming babysitters. So, they dug deep into child development research and discovered the Montisery method. Toys that spark real world learning, not passive scrolling. What started as a parenting fix turned into Claptor, a made in India Montasurer inspired toy brand. Their flagship, a wooden busy board that looks boring until you see a toddler zipping, toggling, flipping and building fine motor skills. No noise, no lights, just pure tactile learning. On Shark Tank India season 4, Clap Store got an all Shark deal. Why? Because they had already hit 2 and a half cr in sales. And they built a brand on product credibility, not just influencer hype. Everything's crafted with eco-friendly wood, non-toxic paint, and no sharp edges to keep kids safe and engaged. Just scream free. Comment toy and I'll DM you the toy that made the sharks bite.

title: Ryan Reynolds is copying this master strategy! #shorts #business #deadpool #ryanreynolds
text:
Ryan Reynolds is copying this master strategy and no one knows its name. 

So Mr Deadpool was getting paid like a million dollars for every advertisement. But he eventually realized that, "I'm just getting peanuts, and the big companies are making huge money."

So he founded his own marketing agency so that there will be no mediator to cut his share.

But it was just the start because the main game started when he used the "Thrasio Style Model" in his business which is nothing but buying fast-growing companies at an early stage.

Now because Ryan has the marketing agency, he created all the ads for his own companies.

And by doing this, he was able to scale companies like Aviation Gin, Mint Mobile, even Wrexham, plus he generated extra money by doing ads for them.

Today the collective value of these companies is $14 billion and he is in the process to buy more companies.

That's why I always say to founders – "Try to go up in the value chain, because there the real cash party is happening."

title: Kareena to Alia - They All Wear This!
This one machine saved Tanishq. 😱|ft. Vargab Bakshi| #shorts #tata #business
text:
TATA was almost shutting down Tanishq, and this one machine saved everything. 

So back in 1998, when the jewelry sector was totally unorganized, people trusted their local jewelers more than any big brand.

And because of this, Tanishq's losses were increasing day by day, and it became very difficult to win people's trust.

So to tackle this, Tanishq introduced a German machine in every store called "karat meter", 
which tests how pure your gold is, and that too for free.

Now people rushed to Tanishq stores to test their gold, and surprisingly, over 60% of gold was of low quality. 

And because of that, people got sad, and this is where Tanishq dropped a bomb deal: Impure to Pure.

So if you have less than 22-carat gold, then go to the Tanishq store. They will replace it with pure 22-carat gold, and this strategy performed like crazy.

That's why I always say to founders: Don't be afraid to expose industry-wide issues if you have a solution to offer.

Share this video with a TATA fan and follow Grow Kwik, India's No.1 D2C channel.

title: This billboard is 100% fake, and “The Rock” can’t do anything! #shorts #business
text:
This billboard is 100% fake, and “The Rock” can’t do anything!

This is “Surreal Cereal,” a cereal brand that had a good product but had no marketing budget to hire celebrities.

So they came up with a unique idea.

Rather than approaching popular celebrities with a small budget, they reached out to normal people that had the same names as celebrities like Dwayne Johnson, Ronaldo, Michael Jordan, and many others.

And as usual, these normal people liked the cereals, and Surreal’s team recorded their video testimonials, saying things like, "This cereal is amazing." – Ronaldo. "This cereal is the best I've ever had." – Dwayne Johnson.

And then they made billboards that said things like, “We’re Dwayne Johnson’s favorite cereal” and “Official cereal of Ronaldo.” 

And guess what? The company generated crazy profit out of it.

Now you may think that it is very similar to what Nike has done at the time of the London Olympics 2012 by showing different cities with the similar name “London.”

That’s why I am asking – is it fair to do these fake campaigns using celebs or place names? Tell us in the comments!

title: 5 Steps Before Selecting Your Business Name 👇 | ft.Vargab Bakshi | #shorts #business
text:
5 Steps Before Selecting Your Business Name -

Let's understand using an example:

We are creating a new company called "Chiku."

Before finalizing the name, there are 5 things we must check:

1. First, check the availability of the domain name with .com and .in extensions.

   It's not available!

   I have an idea: let's put three I's in the name.

   It's available. (High five!)

2. Check on the Ministry of Corporate Affairs website if the name has already been taken by anyone.

   Not taken by anyone. (High five!)

3. Do a public trademark search in the countries where we want to operate in the future.

   It's available in Dubai.

4. Check if "chiiiku" has a foul meaning in any other language in the world.

   Ah, I don't think so.

5. Call my wife: "Hey Chiiiku!"

   Wife: "What?"

   "No, no, I'm just asking if the name 'Chiiiku' is easy to pronounce."

   Wife: "Yes, 'Chiiiku.'"

We're done! Share this video with your friend who wants to start a new business.

title: 5 Steps Before Selecting Your Business Name 👇 | ft.Vargab Bakshi | #shorts #business
text:
5 Steps Before Selecting Your Business Name -

Let's understand using an example:

We are creating a new company called "Chiku."

Before finalizing the name, there are 5 things we must check:

1. First, check the availability of the domain name with .com and .in extensions.

   It's not available!

   I have an idea: let's put three I's in the name.

   It's available. (High five!)

2. Check on the Ministry of Corporate Affairs website if the name has already been taken by anyone.

   Not taken by anyone. (High five!)

3. Do a public trademark search in the countries where we want to operate in the future.

   It's available in Dubai.

4. Check if "chiiiku" has a foul meaning in any other language in the world.

   Ah, I don't think so.

5. Call my wife: "Hey Chiiiku!"

   Wife: "What?"

   "No, no, I'm just asking if the name 'Chiiiku' is easy to pronounce."

   Wife: "Yes, 'Chiiiku.'"

We're done! Share this video with your friend who wants to start a new business.
